body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` **Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` **Triggered by:** Commit 4ebb88df41f3eb4d87efbbb02c2de9b2c3a85db3 (PR #57722) by @live1206 ## What Changed PR #57722 fixed a bug in the management generator's resource detection logic to correctly handle **extension resources that target a specific parent resource type** (e.g., a resource that can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` rather than any arbitrary resource). The fix touched `resource-detection.ts`, `resource-metadata.ts`, and `resolve-arm-resources-converter.ts`. ## Gaps Found - The existing `docs/OperationContext.md` covers extension resources only for the **generic/wildcard scope** case (paths starting with `/{resourceUri}/providers/...`), but does not explain extension resources that specify a **specific parent resource type** via the TypeSpec `@armResourceOperations` scope, nor how the generator detects and differentiates between the two - There is no documentation explaining **how the generator detects whether an extension resource has a specific parent type vs. a wildcard scope**, and what generated code differs between the two cases (e.g., which extension class the resource's helper methods are generated on) - No CHANGELOG file exists in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` to record this bug fix for users of the generator who may have been working around it
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` — **edit** Expand the existing **Extension Resources** subsection (under **Advanced Scenarios**) to cover the specific parent type case. Replace the current section: ````markdown ### Extension Resources Extension resources have a flexible scope (can extend any resource). Their contextual path starts with a single variable segment: ``` /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The system handles these specially, treating `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's Id. ```` with: ````markdown ### Extension Resources Extension resources extend another Azure resource. The generator recognizes two sub-types: #### Wildcard-scope extension resources When an extension resource can extend **any** resource type, the operation paths use a single variable prefix: ``` /{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The system treats `resourceUri` as a contextual parameter derived from the extended resource's `Id`. Helper methods are added to the `ArmClient` mockable extension class so callers can invoke them from any `ArmResource`. #### Specific-parent extension resources When an extension resource is scoped to a **specific parent resource type** (for example, it can only extend `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`), the TypeSpec definition constrains the scope. The detected parent type determines which Azure scope class (e.g., `ResourceGroupResource`, `SubscriptionResource`, `TenantResource`, `ManagementGroupResource`, or the parent resource's own collection/resource class) receives the helper extension methods. The generator distinguishes these cases during resource detection by inspecting whether the extension resource's path prefix resolves to a known Azure scope path rather than a generic variable segment. When a specific parent is detected, the resource's `parentResourceId` in the code model is set to the concrete parent path, enabling the generator to place helper methods on the correct extension class. **Example:** An extension resource with parent type `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`: ``` /subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/{vmName}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName} ``` The generator detects `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines` as the specific parent and adds `GetExtension(string extensionName)` to the `MockableVirtualMachineResource` class, not the generic `ArmClient` extension. ```` #### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` — **create** Create a CHANGELOG file to track generator releases and bug fixes: ```markdown # Release History ## Unreleased ### Bug Fixes - Fixed detection of extension resources that target a specific parent resource type. Previously, extension resources with a constrained scope (e.g., restricted to `Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines`) were not correctly identified as having a specific parent, causing helper methods to be generated on the wrong extension class or missing entirely. ([#57722](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/57722)) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No README snippet changes are needed for this developer-tool documentation update. The docs are prose-only markdown inside `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/`. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is a generator tool README, not an end-user SDK README, so the standard template sections do not strictly apply. However, consider adding a **"Known Limitations / Notes"** or a link to the CHANGELOG once it exists. ### Step 4: Validate This is a documentation-only change (markdown files and a new CHANGELOG). No build or test commands are required beyond a basic review. However, to confirm the generator itself still works: 1. `cd eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt && npm install` 2. `./eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` — re-generates all test projects; should complete with no errors 3. Review `git diff` on the generated test project files to confirm no regressions
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Management Generator] Docs: Document extension resource detection with specific parent types
